No Poverty.reason: International Sales Law facilitates cross-border transactions, which can indirectly drive economic growth and resource mobilization, contributing to poverty reduction (Target 1.a).

Zero Hunger.reason: The legal framework of international sales could apply to agricultural trade, potentially impacting market functions (Target 2.b), but this is not an explicit focus of the course.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: There is no explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus linking International Sales Law to health or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and cultivates professional skills for employment in international trade (Target 4.4).
evidence: 培養同學處理國際貿易的能力

Gender Equality.reason: There is no explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus linking International Sales Law to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: There is no explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus linking International Sales Law to clean water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: There is no explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus linking International Sales Law to energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly supports productive economic activities, job creation, and economic growth by providing the legal framework for cross-border transactions and dispute resolution (Targets 8.1, 8.3).
evidence: 培養同學處理國際貿易的能力

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course establishes the legal 'soft infrastructure' for international trade, enabling enterprises to integrate into global value chains and markets (Targets 9.1, 9.3).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Uniform international trade law (CISG) can reduce legal barriers, promoting more equitable participation in global markets for all countries, including developing ones (Target 10.a).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: There is no explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus linking International Sales Law to sustainable cities or communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core competency B explicitly requires students to 'understand and identify ethical, socially responsible, and sustainability issues,' which applies to international commercial practices (Target 12.6).
evidence: To understand and identify ethical, socially responsible, and sustainability issues

Climate Action.reason: There is no explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus linking International Sales Law directly to climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: There is no explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus linking International Sales Law to marine ecosystems.

Life on Land.reason: There is no explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus linking International Sales Law to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course is fundamentally about establishing and applying international legal frameworks for trade and dispute resolution, directly promoting the rule of law and access to justice (Target 16.3).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course directly supports a rules-based multilateral trading system (Target 17.10) and builds capacity for international cooperation and global partnerships in trade.
